title: polypogon monspeliensis subsp. subspathaceus
intro: Polypogon monspeliensis subsp. subspathaceus, also known as the Mediterranean bent or French meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Polypogon monspeliensis subsp. subspathaceus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
